location.office_time_required: 3 days per week (Tuesdays, Thursdays, and a third flex day); up to 4 weeks remote per year

secondary_archetype: Growth PM with marketplace and martech focus

level_signals: 8+ years of product management experience required
Comp range $220K-$302.5K base, consistent with Staff-level IC at a Series D+ marketplace company
Expected to set multi-year direction, influence at executive level, and recruit A-players

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Direct marketplace experience. You are fluent with the dynamics of competing customer objectives across both sides of the marketplace, you anticipate second-order effects, and you have a track record of finding elusive win-win solutions.

AI-first in your own and your team's workflows, and in what you build. You actively use AI to move faster, ship more, and make your team more effective. You have a clear point of view on how to apply AI to brand-facing tools in wholesale tech and martech, including what is possible now and what is around the corner.
Highly customer-focused, with strong qualitative and quantitative judgment. You make decisions that hold up on both sides of the marketplace, for brands and for retailers.

Strong strategic thinking and execution skills. Excited to set high-level vision and strategy while also finessing customer-experience details, with a clear track record of driving business impact through product.

Proven zero-to-one product experience. You have built and shipped new, customer-facing capabilities from early concept through launch and iteration, with the discipline to set crisp success metrics and drive tight build-learn-iterate cycles.
Structured communicator and systems thinker who can make complexity simple.
Experience leading major launches alongside go-to-market and marketing teams.

Experience with product analytics, A/B testing, long-term holdouts, and modeling out product impact. You pull a coherent picture from a wide range of data types and methods, you acknowledge what you do not yet know, and you are a thoughtful partner and challenger to your data and analytics partners.

Proven leader who influences across functions and at the executive level. You set direction for the full EPDD and GTM team, pull in the right partners, hold them accountable, recruit A-players, and uplevel the people around you.
Owner and builder. You want to meaningfully rebuild and up-level tools and programs, you get into the details, and you hold a high bar for yourself and the team across a large and complex scope.

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Experience in consulting, finance, professional services, or business school can be especially useful preparation for the strategic and cross-functional complexity it involves.
Direct experience in growth, marketing, or CRM is a plus.

scope.surface: Relationship Growth — brand-side tools including retailer tracking/insights, brand-to-retailer communications (email, in-product, messaging), and personalized sales/support tools (lead gen, promos, CRM-like capabilities)
primary_metrics_inferred: Brand engagement and retention on platform
Retailer discovery and conversion driven by brand tools
GMV growth from brand-driven outreach channels
core_responsibilities: Own strategy and roadmap for Relationship Growth
Set the multi-year direction for how Faire's brand-side tools evolve and deliver compounding value
Meaningfully rebuild and up-level tools and programs where the current ceiling is too low
Partner across the full EPDD and GTM team
Shape how brand-driven communications fit within the broader Faire ecosystem
Set the direction for how AI reshapes the wholesale tech and martech tools brands rely on

green_flags: Compensation range explicitly stated and competitive ($220K-$302.5K base)
Clear product surface and ownership described in detail
Role has genuine strategic scope (multi-year roadmap, AI direction-setting)
Company backed by top-tier investors including Sequoia, Y Combinator, Founders Fund
Explicit acknowledgment that the role goes beyond traditional PM — signals maturity of expectations
red_flags: Hybrid requirement is 3 days/week in office (SF or NYC only) — no fully remote option
Visa sponsorship and work authorization not addressed in the JD
Very wide comp range ($82.5K spread) may indicate significant leveling ambiguity or location-based variation
Role is described as 'closer to running a small business inside Faire' — scope and accountability may be underestimated at hiring stage
No explicit headcount or team size mentioned — unclear how much is leading vs. doing

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from title 'Staff PM', comp range, and scope signals)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities; not explicitly listed in JD)
compensation.location_pay_differential (range given for SF/NYC jointly, not separately, so no differential apparent)
ambiguities: The JD lists a single pay range attributed to 'San Francisco, CA/New York City, NY' — it is unclear if this is a combined range applicable to both cities or if there is an unstated differential between the two locations.

The consulting/finance/MBA background item is presented in a way that blurs between must-have and nice-to-have; it is classified as nice-to-have here since the phrasing is 'can be especially useful preparation' rather than a hard requirement.

manages_people is set to false because the JD does not explicitly assign direct reports, but the role is expected to 'recruit A-players' and 'uplevel the people around you', which may imply indirect or future people management.
